conflict: The Carrow regime at its most direct: mass detention, the Cruciatus Curse used on students as collective punishment, and the physical aftermath that costs Gavin hours of work time
consequence: Physical (Cruciatus aftermath, persistent tremor in left hand) and relational (first real fracture with Miren over his apparent indifference to the torture). The comedy of scale becomes visible: Gavin's dominant response to being Cruciated is irritation at the lost evening of translation work
must_land_beats: The Cruciatus must be rendered with full physical specificity — the specific quality of pain without physical origin, the body's response to impossible signals, the aftermath of shaking and metallic taste
Gavin's wrong reaction: anger directed at lost work time rather than at the Carrows. This is the first beat of the novel's dark comedy and it must play deadpan, not winking
The argument with Miren: she thinks he has checked out. He tries to explain. She hears a boy saying his library project matters more than classmates being tortured. She is not entirely wrong
pressure_source: The Carrow regime at its most direct: mass detention, the Cruciatus Curse used on students as collective punishment, and the physical aftermath that costs Gavin hours of work time
